title: Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce

seo.title: Authentic Chicken Mole Recipe | Pollo en Mole Sauce
seo.metaDescription: Master the rich flavors of authentic Mexican Chicken Mole with our easy Pollo en Mole recipe. Learn how to make this classic dish at home with step-by-step instructions. Perfect for dinner!
seo.h1: Authentic Chicken Mole Recipe: Pollo en Mole
h2Headings: What is Chicken Mole (Pollo en Mole)?
Step-by-Step: How to Make Chicken Mole
Tips for the Best Chicken Mole Sauce
Serving Suggestions for Pollo en Mole
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Mexican Chicken Mole (Pollo en Mole) served with rice, garnished with sesame seeds and cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, complex flavors of homemade Chicken Mole, a traditional Mexican dish perfect for any occasion.

description: Pollo en Mole is a quintessential Mexican dish, featuring tender chicken bathed in a rich, complex sauce made from a blend of chiles, spices, nuts, and a hint of chocolate. This authentic recipe guides you through creating a mole poblano that balances sweet, savory, and spicy notes perfectly. It is a celebratory meal that embodies the heart of Mexican culinary tradition.

ingredients.notes: low sodium, plus more if needed

instructions.title: Prepare the Chiles
instructions.text: Heat a dry comal or skillet over medium heat. Toast the dried Ancho, Mulato, and Pasilla chiles for 1-2 minutes per side until fragrant but not burnt. Remove from heat, place in a bowl, cover with hot water, and let rehydrate for 15-20 minutes until soft. Drain, reserving some soaking liquid.

instructions.title: Toast Aromatics and Spices
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil. Add the chopped onion and garlic, cooking until softened, about 3-5 minutes. Add almonds, peanuts, 1 tablespoon of sesame seeds, raisins, and tortilla pieces. Toast until lightly golden and fragrant, about 3-4 minutes. Add the roasted tomatoes and tomatillos, cinnamon stick, cloves, peppercorns, cumin seeds, and oregano. Cook for another 2 minutes.

instructions.title: Blend the Mole Base
instructions.text: Transfer the rehydrated chiles, along with the toasted aromatics, nuts, and spices from the pot, to a blender. Add 1 cup of chicken broth and blend until very smooth. You may need to add a little more chicken broth or reserved chile soaking liquid to achieve a thick, pourable consistency. Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, pressing firmly on the solids to extract all liquid. Discard the solids.

instructions.title: Cook the Mole Sauce
instructions.text: In the same pot or Dutch oven, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil over medium heat. Pour in the strained mole sauce. Cook, stirring constantly to prevent sticking, for about 15-20 minutes until the sauce darkens, thickens, and the oil separates slightly from the sauce. This step is crucial for developing the mole's deep flavor. Stir in the Mexican chocolate until fully melted and incorporated. Add salt to taste and the optional sugar if needed to balance the flavors.

instructions.title: Simmer Chicken in Mole
instructions.text: Add the remaining 3 cups of chicken broth to the mole sauce. Bring to a gentle simmer. Add the chicken pieces to the mole sauce, ensuring they are fully submerged. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking and ensure even cooking.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Once the chicken is cooked, taste the mole sauce and adjust seasoning if necessary. If the sauce is too thick, add a little more chicken broth or water to reach your desired consistency. Serve the Pollo en Mole hot, garnished with the remaining 1 tablespoon of sesame seeds and fresh cilantro, alongside warm rice or tortillas. Enjoy this rich and flavorful Mexican classic!

tips: Toasting your chiles and spices properly is key to developing the deep, complex flavors of mole. Be careful not to burn them, as this will result in a bitter taste.
If you cant find Mexican chocolate, you can use unsweetened dark chocolate (about 70-85% cacao) with a pinch of cinnamon and a tiny bit of ground anise seed for a similar flavor profile.
Mole sauce often tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld. It can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days or frozen for longer.
article: Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce is a beloved Mexican Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
